primary_complaint: Right shoulder pain and weakness following a work-related lifting injury in June 2024
functional_limitations: Unable to lift objects above shoulder height
Cannot perform overhead reaching for daily tasks
Disturbed sleep due to shoulder pain
Unable to return to work duties requiring arm elevation
objective_measurements: Active forward flexion: 95 degrees (limited by pain)
Strength testing: 3/5 supraspinatus on empty can test

conservative_treatments_attempted.outcome: minimal improvement, pain persisted with overhead activity
2 weeks of partial relief, then return to baseline symptoms
inadequate pain control, GI intolerance developed

imaging_findings.key_findings: X-ray (01/22/2025): AC joint arthrosis Grade II. No calcific deposits. No significant glenohumeral arthrosis.; MRI (01/22/2025): Full-thickness supraspinatus tear measuring 2.1 cm with retraction to the musculotendinous junction. Moderate subacromial bursitis. Intact subscapularis.

requested_procedure: Arthroscopic rotator cuff repair, right shoulder — CPT 29827
surgical_approach_if_mentioned: Arthroscopic with mini-open rotator cuff repair

denial_risk_flags.label: Corticosteroid Injection Duration Not Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The corticosteroid injection administered on 11/05/2024 has no documented treatment duration or number of injections attempted. Payers for CPT 29827 often require documentation of at least one injection trial with a defined observation period before approving surgical intervention.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add a chart addendum specifying the observation period following the 11/05/2024 Kenalog injection and confirm whether a single injection was trialed or if repeat injections were considered and declined.

denial_risk_flags.label: Physical Therapy Duration Below Typical Threshold
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Only 8 weeks of physical therapy is documented. Many BCBS plans require a minimum of 6–12 weeks of structured PT with documented failure before approving CPT 29827, and some reviewers may consider 8 weeks borderline given the surgical nature of the request.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add documentation clarifying why further PT was not pursued (e.g., structural tear size, functional decline, provider judgment) and confirm that PT was supervised and formally prescribed.

denial_risk_flags.label: Attending Surgeon Name Is a Placeholder
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The attending surgeon is listed as Dr. Michael Torres, which is a placeholder token. Payers require a clearly identified requesting and operating provider for prior authorization review.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Ensure the actual attending surgeon name is populated in the final submission. The requesting provider field must match the operating surgeon on the authorization request.

denial_risk_flags.label: Imaging Date Is a Placeholder
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Both MRI and X-ray dates are listed as 01/22/2025, a placeholder token. Payers require confirmed imaging dates to verify that imaging was obtained within an acceptable pre-authorization window (typically within 6–12 months).
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Replace 01/22/2025 with the actual imaging date before submission to confirm imaging recency and compliance with BCBS imaging currency requirements.

denial_risk_flags.label: Injection Date Is a Placeholder
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The corticosteroid injection date is listed as 11/05/2024, a placeholder token. Payers require specific dates for all conservative treatments to verify the timeline of failed conservative care.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Replace 11/05/2024 with the actual injection date to establish a complete and verifiable conservative care timeline for BCBS review.

diagnosis_codes.note: Two ICD-10 codes are documented (M75.121 and M75.111), clearly supporting the diagnosis of full-thickness rotator cuff tear and shoulder impingement.

conservative_treatments_named.note: Three distinct conservative treatments are named: Physical Therapy, Corticosteroid Injection (Kenalog), and NSAIDs (Meloxicam).

conservative_treatment_duration.note: Physical therapy (8 weeks) and NSAIDs (3 months) have documented durations, but the corticosteroid injection has no documented duration or observation period, leaving the conservative care timeline incomplete.

imaging_findings.note: Both MRI and X-ray are documented as completed with detailed findings including tear size, retraction, and bursitis, strongly supporting surgical candidacy.

functional_limitations.note: Four specific functional limitations are explicitly documented, including inability to perform overhead work, sleep disturbance, and inability to return to work duties.

surgical_approach.note: Surgical approach is explicitly stated as arthroscopic with mini-open rotator cuff repair.

cpt_code_valid.note: CPT 29827 (arthroscopic rotator cuff repair) is correctly matched to the documented diagnosis of full-thickness rotator cuff tear.

symptom_duration.note: Symptom duration is documented as 8 months of failed conservative management, with injury onset noted as June 2024.
